step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous assessment that focusing specifically on reducing the maxillary supporting cusp is an effective way to minimize non-working side interference. The maxilla often has more pronounced anatomical features such as canines which tend to be larger and thus contribute significantly to interferences during occlusion. By targeting these areas, we achieve better precision in correcting any potential issues without overly altering other parts of the dentition.

Answer Choice: A. ```markdown
